senses.gloss: Tigris (a river in West Asia flowing 1,150 miles east-southeast from the Armenian Highland in Turkey through Iraq)

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Sumerian 𒀀𒇉 (id₂ /⁠id⁠/)
Sumerian 𒉌 (i₃ /⁠i⁠/)
Sumerian 𒁺 (g̃en)
Sumerian 𒀀 (a /⁠'a⁠/)
?
Sumerian 𒀀𒇉𒈦𒄘𒃼 (^(id₂)idigna /⁠Idigna⁠/)bor.
Akkadian 𒀀𒇉𒈦𒄘𒃼 (Idiglat)bor.
Aramaic דיגלת (diḡlāṯ)bor.
Arabic دجلة
Borrowed from Aramaic דיגלת (diḡlāṯ), borrowed from Akkadian 𒀀𒇉𒈦𒄘𒃼 (Idiglat), borrowed from Sumerian 𒀀𒇉𒈦𒄘𒃼 (^(id₂)idigna /⁠Idigna⁠/), possibly from 𒀀𒇉 (id₂ /⁠id⁠/, “river”) + 𒉌 (i₃ /⁠i⁠/, finite prefix) + 𒁺 (g̃en, “went”) + 𒀀 (a /⁠'a⁠/, nominalising suffix).
